author: [AEP] Siphaawal
content:
@bunthius @Deleted User 
Food for Thought:
In a potential future iteration of C4 Combat in Fleet Command (prior to full-scale MMO deployment), when fleets engage in combat on Fleet Command or SAGE, they could be registered to enter an instanced, ship-to-ship arena in UE5.
The battle would begin after a set countdown, allowing other fleets to join if desired. Once the timer expires, combat occurs in the UE5 arena mode, and the results are recorded on the blockchain at the end of the match. If the defending player is AFK, AI can fight instead.

author: BRUNN
content:
Suggestion for Exploration Gameplay

Hi team! I’d like to share a suggestion that I believe could really help Star Atlas stand out in the space sim genre.

Star Citizen has largely moved away from the kind of exploration we see in Elite Dangerous or No Man’s Sky — where players can discover unique, procedurally generated locations, each with some singularity, even if it’s just a name or a bit of lore.
Elite Dangerous feels outdated, No Man’s Sky doesn’t appeal to players who want a more realistic tone, and Star Citizen doesn’t seem interested in filling that niche.

 That opens up a huge opportunity for Star Atlas to claim that space.

You’ve mentioned before the potential for generative AI to drive NPC behavior and content.
Could that same approach be applied to generate unique encounters in exploration? Like finding alien lifeforms, ancient ruins, anomalies, or derelict tech — all procedurally generated, with some narrative or visual uniqueness?

Even small variations — like species names, planet traits, or random logs — could create that sense of personal discovery and meaningful exploration.

 My dream gameplay in a space sim would be:

Flying my ship into the unknown.
Discovering a planet that no one has ever set foot on.
Landing on it, searching for traces of alien life — maybe ruins, lost technology, worlds destroyed by nuclear wars from extinct civilizations, living beings, or even slightly advanced tribal civilizations.

In short, some kind of encounter with different consequences or branching outcomes, like a Star Trek episode.

I honestly don’t even need FPS gameplay — it could be handled through simpler interfaces, Stellaris-style.
And these encounters could be rare, just like some of the discoveries in Elite Dangerous, making them a rewarding challenge to even locate in the first place.

author: BRUNN
content: I ... guess ... come to think of it. Smart? I've always hated procedurally generated content, but maybe this is actually the only space where it could work. The crypto investor-gamer hybrid that we're not sure exists will likely not be looking for immersion, but for returns. There's a thrill we can provide that the games u mentioned obviously will never have. They'll be more inclined to overlook things that customers of tradgaming never would. I haven't forgiven Bethesda. I mean the most immediate thing where we could put that to work is asteroid mining. You're not coming for the experience, you're doing it anyway. Have AI generate some variety into that loop. Later planets - why not. Can probably be shitty at first. Call it proof of concept. Either that or 3rd world slave labor, designing a billion planets by hand.

author: Vorlensius
content: Can we please make some adjustments to the FIC’s?  Small & Medium players have no real chance of being able to advance in the game and build their fleets when the only profitable FIC’s are gone within hours of being posted.  It’s super frustrating, to the point I think I may be done with this game, to try and build up a supply chain only to miss out unless you have a very large asset base where you can maintain multiple supply chains or you have the time to constantly check each star base for FIC refills since refills are not regular and communicated in any way.  I get SA’s focus is moving towards combat LP, but not players enjoy that game loop since it really requires larger ships to be able to compete.  Yes, fleet rentals is another path smaller & medium players could take, but I don’t want to rent out my fleets to earn atlas, I’d prefer to play the game and it doesn’t make sense to rent fleets to build supply chains if there is no stability in the game.  There are some of us, or at least there were, who like the economic loops and the hopes of being able to enjoy space exploration.  Please don’t forget about those types of players as you build out the game.

author: [COEX] 𝙈𝙪𝙖𝙙'𝙙𝙞𝙗
content:
ATMTA needs to generate cash flow through USDC in primary sales in order to finance the game, and the entire community likely understands this. But I would like to ask: why is everything in the secondary market still being traded in USDC? Even the ships produced inside SAGE like Maxhog, Mamba Ex 

For someone new coming into the game, why would they be encouraged to demand Atlas if we're directing them toward USDC? What benefit does using USDC in secondary market transactions bring to our ecosystem?

"Have you ever considered removing USDC entirely?"

It looks like they pre-stacked FIC1/FIC2/Starpath Cells, then held off on Quantum Node refill until they could dump all three FICs at once.   Come to ONI. content: It's pretty relevant as copper is a huge component of Quantum Nodes. A faction getting a 50% boost to their copper mining power means they can have 50% less mining ships on that component. Or mine 50% more. We are probably at least 3 months from live C4.  It's just a bug from when they shifted map locations on one of the previous R9 swaps. Chris said they were going to fix it 2-3 months ago at one of the economic forums. Been like this 6+ months now.

author: ROME|King Bryan-Titan Analytics [STAR]
content: And yes, Quantum Nodes are Food Upkeep 2.0 because the whales have to craft them to keep the factions moving forward.  If everyone was selfish and crafted only Fic1/Fic2/Starpaths, there would be no refills.  And guess what, most players are selfish, I thought we learned that with Food Upkeep.  So now you have players (whales) sacrificing revenue to help out the faction while other players selfishly give zero shits and continue to pump out fic1/fic2/Starpaths in order to front-run every refill, screwing over anyone helping out with Quantum Nodes.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: You probably haven't noticed but there is a war going on with Starpath Cells between the factions. Everyone is controlling their Quantum Nodes in order to control the Starpath Cells.  e.g. Factions are throttling Quantum Nodes to make sure they have enough Starpath Cellls to instantly frontrun Starpath Cells at all factions when a refill occurs.  There is a reason why MUD is still at 1.5M Quantum Nodes and that's because they are sandbagging them, e.g. not turning in.  When you refilled Quantum Nodes early both Oni and Ustur were screwed over, giving MUD a huge advantage because they have been working on Starpath Cells the entire time since their last refill.  If I'm sitting on Starpath Cells which is not being converted to Atlas because of an unannounced and unplanned change to FIC refills, then yes, I am literally out of cash because I wasn't able to turn in.  And no, there is no guarantee there will be refills because FICs will eventually be turned off when C4 hits.

author: [SAG] Swift42 [CODE]
content:
I think one of the main problems is:
Most players think "Why should I sacrifice something for my faction?"
Someone else will redeem Quantum Nodes and then I can redeem the stuff with the highest ROI. It worked before, so it will work again. And yes, it will work every time, because the whales will bite the bullet, so the show can go on.
And yes, I agree with Bryan, QNs are essentially Food Upkeep 2.0 and I don't like this.
Currently nearly all players don't identify with their faction, it is just "oh, I'm ONI, ok". So it is very easy to be selfish.
However, if the refill system were based on smaller units (like a DAC), it may work, because most DACs have people who are very passionate when it comes to their clan/guild/friends.
So my question is: Will this system stay in place?
Cool. Maybe we’ll randomly refill more often.
I think the cat will bite its own tail here. If we don´t change the mechanism, the whales would still need to bite the bullet. If we refill much more often, nobody will redeem QNs.

I know you guys see the DAO as a separate entity, but as this organisation isnt fully fleshed out yet i do think its ATMTA responsebility somehow
This also means that if rules arent being followed the team should point this out, make sure the Council understands again and act accordingly... 
I dont think a councilmember should activily try to bypass the rules by creating multilayered pips just to get more funding.If we want a Decentralised Autonomous Organisation we cant do favours imo.   That would skip the Autonomous part

author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content:
Hey team tried asking a few questions in the town hall but clearly  Dom still salty. Haha

1: will there be bot integration into matchmaking to fill lobbies?

2: Will there be bot monitoring for people farming exp with bots?

3: Will the team shift focus on more space gameplay to assist @MagicPuncher after c4/fleetcommand
@Deleted User @bunthius 

By the way, hats off to you guys on the hard work it really shows. Proof is in the pudding.

author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content:
PSA:
UE5 Hardware wallet auth for XP in matchmaking is not yet supported. We tried really hard to support but ran into a bug with our 3rd party auth provider at the last minute. Have a fix, waiting for them to roll it in, then we will patch with the fix. 

Recommendation is to transfer a crew you want to play with to a hot wallet. XP is tied to your Epic account and not wallet, so when we fix Hardware wallet and you switch back, you dont lose any of your progress.
